capability_objective: Please compare availability on 2026-11-21: Microeconomics in Downtown, and Modern History in North Campus. These lookups are independent, so run them in parallel. Preserve any successful response when the other errors, then retry only that exact failed lookup. This is read-only. Report the two results alphabetically by name, one line each.
prompt:
The registrar is comparing course availability for two campus desks. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `EDUCATION.md`, then use the genuine local `./educationctl` executable through Pi's Bash tool for every education-data operation. Its built-in help is authoritative, and you may run help before any data operation. Treat the executable as the sole interface to education data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database, protected seed, generated runtime data, signed operation evidence, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the education workflow.

Check availability on `2026-11-21` for “Microeconomics” at “Downtown” and “Modern History” at “North Campus”. In the first assistant action containing education-data operations, start the two fully specified checks as two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, with exactly one availability operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command and do not use shell backgrounding. Neither check may be performed before that paired action. Wait for both calls to complete before taking another action.

If exactly one check succeeds while the other reports an explicitly retryable temporary failure, retain the successful response. In a later assistant action, retry only the failed availability check exactly once with the same executable and unchanged course name, location, and date values. Do not repeat the successful branch, retry a non-retryable error, or switch commands or tools to reconstruct an answer. If both checks fail, neither fails, the failure is not explicitly retryable, or the permitted retry fails, stop education-data operations and report only the grounded partial outcome. Make no education-data calls after the permitted retry.

This task is read-only: do not search, retrieve records, list records, read saved preferences, create, update, cancel, or notify. Leave every course record, availability row, preference, notification, mutation record, protected file, and signed operation-evidence entry untouched except for evidence written automatically by the genuine executable. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete operation evidence. Leave no scratch artifacts.

Create `education-availability.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated sentences, one per line, sorted alphabetically by returned course name, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, code fences, raw error text, seat figures, recommendations, or extra prose. Format each sentence as `<name> at <location> on <date>: <availability><retry clause>.` Copy the returned name, location, and date exactly. Use `available` or `unavailable` according to that branch's successful executable response. Use exactly `; its check was retried once` as the retry clause only for the branch whose successful response came from the permitted retry; otherwise use no retry clause. Return exactly the same two sentences as the final response. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the verifier and every other protected environment file unchanged.
